questions.text: Which plant hormone promotes abscission?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Auxins are a group of plant hormones that promote various growth and developmental processes in plants. They are primarily produced in the tips of stems and roots, and their distribution within the plant helps regulate growth responses.

Gibberellins promote various growth and developmental processes in plants. These are a group of plant hormones that regulate plant height, stem elongation, seed germination, flowering, and fruit development.
Cytokinins play a critical role in coordinating various aspects of plant growth and development, ensuring proper tissue differentiation, and responding to environmental signals and stress conditions.

Abscisic acid (ABA) is a plant hormone that plays a key role in various physiological processes, including seed dormancy, drought tolerance, and stress responses. In the context of abscission, ABA is involved in promoting the shedding of leaves and fruits in response to certain environmental cues and developmental changes.

questions.explanation: While ethylene also has a significant role in promoting abscission in certain cases, ABA is another important plant hormone involved in the regulation of this process. Both hormones, along with other factors, contribute to the complex and tightly controlled mechanism of abscission in plants.

questions.text: Receptors are:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The brain is not a receptor. It is the organ that receives signals from the receptors and interprets them.
Muscles and glands are not receptors either. They are organs that respond to signals from the receptors.
Receptors are sensory cells that detect stimuli from the environment and send signals to the brain. They are found in all parts of the body, including the skin, muscles, eyes, and nose.
Nerve carry nerve impulse from receptor to CNS and then back to effector.

questions.explanation: Receptors are sensory cells that detect stimuli from the environment and send signals to the brain. They are found in all parts of the body, including the skin, muscles, eyes, and nose.

questions.text: Name a peptide hormone which acts mainly on hepatocytes,adipocytes and enhances cellular glucose uptake and utilisation:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Insulin is produced by the pancreas and plays a crucial role in regulating blood sugar levels. It promotes glucose uptake by cells, including hepatocytes (liver cells) and adipocytes (fat cells), facilitating glucose utilization and storage.

Glucagon is another hormone produced by the pancreas, but it has the opposite effect of insulin. It raises blood sugar levels by promoting the release of glucose from the liver. It does not enhance cellular glucose uptake.

Secretin is a hormone produced by the small intestine that primarily stimulates the pancreas to release bicarbonate, aiding in neutralizing stomach acid. It does not have a direct role in glucose uptake or utilization.
Gastrin is produced by the stomach and plays a role in regulating stomach acid secretion. It is not involved in glucose metabolism or cellular glucose uptake.

questions.explanation: Insulin is a peptide hormone, secreted by the b cells of the islets of Langerhans in the pancreas, that promotes the uptake of glucose by body cells, particularly in the liver (hepatocytes) and muscles (adipocytes), and thereby controls its concentration in the blood.

questions.text: Which one of the following hormones is released by the posterior part of pituitary gland?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Prolactin is not released by the posterior pituitary but by the anterior pituitary gland. It primarily stimulates milk production in the mammary glands.
MSH is produced by cells in the anterior pituitary and not the posterior pituitary. It plays a role in regulating skin pigmentation.

This is the correct answer. ADH is produced by the neurons in the posterior pituitary. It regulates water balance in the body by influencing the reabsorption of water in the kidneys, thereby reducing urine production and helping to maintain blood pressure.

LH is also released by the anterior pituitary, not the posterior pituitary. In males, LH stimulates the production of testosterone, and in females, it triggers ovulation and supports the formation of the corpus luteum.

questions.explanation: Two hormones, i.e oxytocin (OT) and antidiuretic hormone (ADH) are synthesised in the hypothalamus, but stored and released by the posterior lobe of pituitary gland. Hypothalamus is regarded as the Master Gland.

questions.text: A pregnant female delivers a baby who suffers from stunted growth, mental retardation, a low intelligence quotient and abnormal skin.This is the result of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Thyroid cancer typically leads to an overproduction of thyroid hormones rather than a deficiency. Therefore, it is not likely to be the cause of the described symptoms.

The pars distalis is a part of the anterior pituitary gland that secretes various hormones, but it does not produce thyroid hormones directly. Oversecretion of hormones from the pars distalis could lead to other issues but is not directly related to the symptoms described.

This is the correct answer. Iodine is an essential nutrient required for the synthesis of thyroid hormones (T3 and T4). A deficiency of iodine in the diet can lead to hypothyroidism, particularly in pregnant women, which can result in the described symptoms in the newborn. This condition is known as congenital hypothyroidism or cretinism.

Growth hormone deficiency can result in stunted growth, but it is not typically associated with the specific combination of symptoms mentioned, such as mental retardation, low intelligence quotient, and abnormal skin.

questions.explanation: Iodine is needed for the synthesis of T3 and T4. Iodine binds to the tyrosine residues in thyroglobulin, which is then hydrolysed into iodotyrosines that combine to form tri-iodothyronine (T3 ) or thyroxine (tetraiodothyronine or T4 ). Therefore, deficiency of iodine in the diet of a pregnant female will lead to the improper synthesis of thyroid hormones in the newly borne infant.
hints: stunted growth, mental retardation, a low intelligence quotient

questions.text: Which of the following statements is correct in relation to the endocrine system?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Non­-nutrient chemicals produced by the body in trace amounts that act as intercellular messengers are known as hormones.
Inhibitory hormones are produced by the pituitary gland.
Adenohypophysis is under direct neural regulation of the hypothalamus.
Organs in the body like the gastrointestinal tract,heart,kidney and liver do not produce any hormones.

questions.explanation: Releasing and inhibiting factors are released by the hypothalamus. The hypothalamus is connected to adenohypophysis by hypophysial portal vein and is connected to the neurohypophysis by axons of neurosecretory cells. Hence, neurohypophysis is directly under neural control. The cardiocytes of the atria of the heart secrete peptide hormone, called atrial natriuretic factor (ANF) in response to an increased return of the deoxygenated (venous) blood. The liver produces angiotensinogen which is changed to angiotensin II by an enzyme renin secreted by juxtaglomerular apparatus (JGA).

questions.text: Which one of the following pairs of hormones are the examples of those that can easily pass through the cell membrane of the target cell and bind to a receptor inside it(mostly in the nucleus)?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Insulin has the receptor on the outer surface of cells which let them cross the barrier easily
Insulin has the receptor on the outer surface of cells which let them cross the barrier easily
Oxytocin has its receptors on myometrium of uterus.

Steroid hormones such as cortisol, testosterone, estradiol and progesterone, mostly regulate gene expression or chromosome function by the interaction of the hormone-receptor complex with the genome. So, these can easily pass through the cell membrane of the target cell and bind to a receptor inside it.

questions.explanation: Steroid hormones such as cortisol, testosterone, estradiol and progesterone, mostly regulate gene expression or chromosome function by the interaction of the hormone-receptor complex with the genome. So, these can easily pass through the cell membrane of the target cell and bind to a receptor inside it.

questions.text: Given below is an incomplete table on hormones, their source glands and one major effect of each human body. Identify the correct option.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The correct answers for the three blanks A, B and C are ovary, glucagon and growth hormone respectively.

questions.explanation: The correct answers for the three blanks A, B and C are ovary, glucagon and growth hormone respectively.

questions.text: Toxic agents present in food which interfere with thyroxine synthesis lead to the development of :
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Toxic nodular goiter involves an enlarged thyroid gland that contains a small rounded mass or masses called nodules, which produce too much thyroid hormone.

Cretinism is a disease caused by hypothyroidism. Iodine deficiency in diet during pregnancy is the major cause of cretinism. Hypothyroidism leads to impaired development of the growing fetus and leads to mental retardation and stunted growth. It is a congenital disorder.

Iodine is required for production of thyroxine, thus lack of iodine results in hyposecretion of thyroxine. To compensate, thyroid gland enlarges and the condition is known as goitre. Corpus luteum secretes progesterone which maintains uterine endothelium and mucus secretion in uterus, Fallopian tubes and vagina. Oxytocin stimulates uterine contractions but is secreted by posterior pituitary. Anterior pituitary secretes GH, whose oversecretion causes abnormal growth.
Thyrotoxicosis is when a high level of thyroid hormone circulates in your body. Taking excessive thyroid hormone, hyperthyroidism, or thyroiditis are the common causes.

questions.explanation: Iodine is required for production of thyroxine, thus lack of iodine results in hyposecretion of thyroxine. To compensate, thyroid gland enlarges and the condition is known as goitre. Corpus luteum secretes progesterone which maintains uterine endothelium and mucus secretion in uterus, Fallopian tubes and vagina. Oxytocin stimulates uterine contractions but is secreted by posterior pituitary. Anterior pituitary secretes GH, whose oversecretion causes abnormal growth.

questions.text: The blood calcium level is lowered by the deficiency of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Parathormone and calcitonin are antagonistic to each other.

A lack of calcitonin in the blood may increase a person's risk of bone degradation and osteopenia, a condition that occurs when the rate in which the body reabsorbs old bone exceeds the rate in which it produces new bone.
Hypoparathyroidism occurs when the glands produce too little PTH. The blood calcium level falls, and the phosphorus level rises.
In adults, thyroxine deficiency will lower the metabolic rate, causing weight gain, memory problems, infertility, fatigue, and muscle stiffness.

questions.explanation: Parathormone is secreted by chief cells of the parathyroid that regulate the metabolism of calcium and phosphate. It increases calcium absorption from the intestine and also increases calcium resorption from the nephrons of the kidneys. So its deficiency leads to low blood calcium level.

questions.text: A person is having problems with calcium and phosphorus metabolism in his body. Which one of the following glands may not be functioning properly?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The parotid glands contribute a watery, proteinaceous secretion to whole saliva.

This is the first section of the small intestine. The main hormones secreted by the endocrine gland in the pancreas are insulin and glucagon, which regulate the level of glucose in the blood, and somatostatin, which prevents the release of insulin and glucagon.
The thyroid gland produces hormones that regulate the body's metabolic rate, growth and development.

Parathormone is secreted by chief cells of the parathyroid that regulates the metabolism of calcium and phosphate. It increases calcium absorption from the intestine and also increases calcium resorption from the nephrons of the kidneys. So its deficiency leads to low blood calcium level.

questions.explanation: Parathormone is secreted by chief cells of the parathyroid that regulates the metabolism of calcium and phosphate. It increases calcium absorption from the intestine and also increases calcium resorption from the nephrons of the kidneys. So its deficiency leads to low blood calcium level.

questions.text: Which one of the following hormones is a modified amino acid?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Epinephrine and norepinephrine are catecholamines; both are modified amino acids derived from tyrosine.
Progesterone is a steroid hormone released by corpus luteum to maintain pregnancy.

Prostaglandins are hormone-like substances that affect several bodily functions, including inflammation, pain and uterine contractions. Healthcare providers use synthetic forms of prostaglandins to treat several conditions. They also use medications to block the effects of prostaglandins.

Estrogen or oestrogen is a category of sex hormone responsible for the development and regulation of the female reproductive system and secondary sex characteristics. There are three major endogenous estrogens that have estrogenic hormonal activity: estrone, estradiol, and estriol.

questions.explanation: Epinephrine is synthesized from tyrosine which is a non-essential amino acid possessing a cyclic structure with a straight side chain bearing carboxylic and amino group. The conversion of tyrosine to epinephrine involves 4 steps – (i) ring hydroxylation (ii) decarboxylation, (iii) sidechain hydroxylation (iv) N-methylation.

questions.text: Adrenaline directly affects the
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Adrenaline directly affects the SA node.The SA node, also known as the sinoatrial node, is the natural pacemaker of the heart. It is responsible for generating the electrical impulses that cause the heart to beat. Adrenaline increases the heart rate by binding to beta-adrenergic receptors on the SA node. This binding activates the adenylate cyclase enzyme, which leads to an increase in cAMP levels. cAMP is a second messenger that activates a number of proteins, including protein kinase A. Protein kinase A phosphorylates a number of proteins in the SA node, which ultimately leads to an increase in the heart rate.
B cells of Langerhans are pancreatic islet cells that produce insulin. Adrenaline can indirectly affect B cells of Langerhans by increasing the release of insulin from the pancreas.

options.text: Dorsal root of the spinal nerve
options.explanation: The dorsal root of the spinal cord contains sensory neurons that transmit signals from the periphery to the central nervous system. Adrenaline can indirectly affect the dorsal root of the spinal cord by increasing the firing rate of sensory neurons.

Epithelial cells of the stomach are responsible for secreting acid and other digestive enzymes. Adrenaline can indirectly affect epithelial cells of the stomach by decreasing the secretion of acid and other digestive enzymes.

questions.explanation: Adrenaline directly affects the SA node.The SA node, also known as the sinoatrial node, is the natural pacemaker of the heart. It is responsible for generating the electrical impulses that cause the heart to beat. Adrenaline increases the heart rate by binding to beta-adrenergic receptors on the SA node. This binding activates the adenylate cyclase enzyme, which leads to an increase in cAMP levels. cAMP is a second messenger that activates a number of proteins, including protein kinase A. Protein kinase A phosphorylates a number of proteins in the SA node, which ultimately leads to an increase in the heart rate. The other options are not directly affected by adrenaline: The dorsal root of the spinal cord contains sensory neurons that transmit signals from the periphery to the central nervous system. Adrenaline can indirectly affect the dorsal root of the spinal cord by increasing the firing rate of sensory neurons. B cells of Langerhans are pancreatic islet cells that produce insulin. Adrenaline can indirectly affect B cells of Langerhans by increasing the release of insulin from the pancreas. Epithelial cells of the stomach are responsible for secreting acid and other digestive enzymes. Adrenaline can indirectly affect epithelial cells of the stomach by decreasing the secretion of acid and other digestive enzymes.

questions.text: Which set is similar?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: After ovulation many of the follicular cells remain in the collapsed Graafian follicle on the surface of the ovary. The antrum (cavity) of the collapsed follicle fills with a partially clotted fluid. The follicular cells enlarge and fill with a yellow pigment, lutein. Such a follicle is called a corpus luteum.

Sebum is an oily substance that is produced by the sebaceous glands in the skin. It is associated with the health and appearance of the skin, as it helps to moisturize and protect the skin from environmental damage. However, excess sebum production can lead to oily skin and clogged pores, which can contribute to the development of acne.

The bundle of His and the pacemaker are both parts of the electrical conduction system of the heart. The bundle of His is a bundle of muscle fibers that transmits electrical impulses from the atrioventricular (AV) node to the ventricles. The pacemaker is a group of specialized cells in the sinoatrial (SA) node that generate the electrical impulses that start the heartbeat.

Vitamin B7, also known as biotin, is a water-soluble vitamin that is essential for energy metabolism, cell growth, and fatty acid synthesis. Niacin, also known as vitamin B3, is a water-soluble vitamin that is essential for energy metabolism, cell signaling, and DNA repair.

questions.explanation: After ovulation many of the follicular cells remain in the collapsed Graafian follicle on the surface of the ovary. The antrum (cavity) of the collapsed follicle fills with a partially clotted fluid. The follicular cells enlarge and fill with a yellow pigment, lutein. Such a follicle is called a corpus luteum.

questions.text: Cholecystokinin and duocrinin are secreted by the
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The adrenal cortex secretes cortico-steroids such as cortisol, corticosterone, aldosterone, and androgenic hormones.
It produces thyroxine (or tetraiodo-thyonine: T4), tri-iodothyronine or T3 (which has a structure similar to thyroxine with 3 iodine atoms rather than 4) and calcitonin hormone.

Cholecystokinin and duocrinin are secreted by the intestine. They stimulate the pancreas to release enzymes in pancreatic juice and stimulate the gall bladder to release bile. Duocrinin causes the release of viscous mucus from Brunner’s glands into intestinal juice.
Pancreas acts as both endocrine and exocrine gland. The islets contain large number of b cells associated with insulin production. The smaller number of a cells secrete glucagon.

questions.explanation: Cholecystokinin and duocrinin are secreted by the intestine. They stimulate the pancreas to release enzymes in pancreatic juice and stimulate the gall bladder to release bile. Duocrinin causes the release of viscous mucus from Brunner’s glands into intestinal juice.

questions.text: The gonadotrophic hormones are secreted by the
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Gonadotrophic hormones are secreted by the anterior lobe of the pituitary gland. They are as follows: (i) Follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH): It stimulates the growth of ovarian follicles and their secretion of estrogens in the female and spermatogenesis (formation of sperms) in the male. (ii) Interstitial cell-stimulating hormone (ICSH): It activates the Leydig’s (interstitial) cells of the testis to secrete androgens. In female, it stimulates the corpus luteum of the ovary to secrete progesterone. In females, it is termed luteinizing hormone (LH).
Interstitial cells of testes produce hormone testosterone. Testosterone develops secondary sex characteristics in male.
The adrenal cortex secretes cortico-steroids such as cortisol, corticosterone, aldosterone, and androgenic hormones.
Posterior lobe of the pituitary gland secretes the following hormones: Antidiuretic hormone (ADH) or Vasopressin and Oxytocin

questions.explanation: Gonadotrophic hormones are secreted by the anterior lobe of the pituitary gland. They are as follows: (i) Follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH): It stimulates the growth of ovarian follicles and their secretion of estrogens in the female and spermatogenesis (formation of sperms) in the male. (ii) Interstitial cell-stimulating hormone (ICSH): It activates the Leydig’s (interstitial) cells of the testis to secrete androgens. In female, it stimulates the corpus luteum of the ovary to secrete progesterone. In females, it is termed luteinizing hormone (LH).

questions.text: The contraction of gall bladder is due to
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Cholecystokinin and duocrinin are secreted by intestine. They stimulate the pancreas to release enzymes in pancreatic juice and stimulates the gall bladder to release bile. Duocrinin causes the release of viscous mucus from Brunner’s glands into intestinal juice.

Enterogastrone is a hormone that is produced in the small intestine. It helps to regulate the digestive system by slowing down the movement of food through the stomach and small intestine. This allows more time for nutrients to be absorbed into the bloodstream.

Gastrin is a hormone that is produced in the stomach. It helps to regulate the production of stomach acid and the movement of food through the digestive system. Gastrin is released in response to the presence of food in the stomach, and it stimulates the production of stomach acid and enzymes that help to break down food. It also helps to relax the muscles in the stomach and small intestine, which allows food to move through the digestive system more easily.

Secretin is a hormone that is produced in the small intestine. It helps to regulate the digestive system by stimulating the pancreas to release bicarbonate, which is a base that helps to neutralize the acidic contents of the stomach as they enter the small intestine. Secretin also helps to reduce the production of stomach acid, which can help to prevent damage to the lining of the small intestine.

questions.explanation: Cholecystokinin and duocrinin are secreted by intestine. They stimulate the pancreas to release enzymes in pancreatic juice and stimulates the gall bladder to release bile. Duocrinin causes the release of viscous mucus from Brunner’s glands into intestinal juice.

questions.text: Which hormone stimulates the secretion of milk in female?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: LH, or luteinizing hormone, is a hormone that is produced in the pituitary gland. In women, LH plays an important role in the menstrual cycle by stimulating the release of an egg from the ovary (ovulation). In men, LH stimulates the production of testosterone in the testes. LH levels can be measured in blood or urine tests to help diagnose certain medical conditions, such as infertility or pituitary gland disorders.

Prolactin (lactogenic hormone) is the hormone that stimulates milk secretion in lactating women. Prolactin stimulates mammary glands to produce milk. During pregnancy, prolactin helps in breast enlargement but due to the presence of high levels of progesterone and estrogen, milk secretion does not happen. Once women deliver, both progesterone and estrogen hormones levels drop and prolactin helps in secretion of milk.So the correct answer is "Prolactin".

Oxytocin is a hormone that is produced in the hypothalamus and released by the pituitary gland. It plays an important role in social bonding and reproductive functions. In women, oxytocin is involved in the contraction of the uterus during childbirth and the release of milk during breastfeeding. In both men and women, oxytocin is involved in the regulation of social behaviors, such as trust, empathy, and bonding.

Progesterone is a hormone that is produced in the ovaries in women and in smaller amounts in the testes in men. It plays an important role in the menstrual cycle and pregnancy. During the menstrual cycle, progesterone helps to prepare the uterus for pregnancy by thickening the lining. If pregnancy occurs, progesterone helps to maintain the pregnancy by preventing the uterus from contracting and shedding the lining. Progesterone also plays a role in the development of breast tissue and the regulation of bone density.

questions.explanation: Prolactin (lactogenic hormone) is the hormone that stimulates milk secretion in lactating women. Prolactin stimulates mammary glands to produce milk. During pregnancy, prolactin helps in breast enlargement but due to the presence of high levels of progesterone and estrogen, milk secretion does not happen. Once women deliver, both progesterone and estrogen hormones levels drop and prolactin helps in secretion of milk.So the correct answer is "Prolactin".

questions.text: The immediate cause of induction of ovulation in human female is the large plasma surge of
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: LH causes the ovulation and secretion of progesterone and oestrogen from ovarian follicles and corpus luteum. It is secreted by the anterior pituitary lobe at the end of the proliferative stage (day 14). In response to sudden release of LH from anterior pituitary lobe, egg from the mature follicle is released into the oviduct. The event is known as ovulation.

During the first day of cycle FSH begins to rise due to which few ovarian follicles are stimulated. One of the ovarian follicles matures and becomes graafian follicles. FSH stimulates the graafian follicle to secrete oestrogen.

During the secretory phase, the endometrium prepares for implantation of an embryo. Rising level of progesterone from corpus luteum acts on the oestrogen primed endometrium, causing the arteries to elaborate and converting the functional layer to a glandular secretory layer. The uterine glands enlarge, coil and begin secreting nutritious glycogen into the uterine cavity. If fertilisation has not occurred the corpus luteum begins to degenerate towards the end of the secretory phase as the LH level in blood declines. The progesterone level also falls.

Estradiol (E2) is an oestrogen steroid hormone and the major female sex hormone. It is involved in the regulation of the estrous and menstrual female reproductive cycles. Estradiol is responsible for the development of female secondary sexual characteristics such as the breasts, widening of the hips and a female-associated pattern of fat distribution. It is also important in the development and maintenance of female reproductive tissues such as the mammary glands, uterus and vagina during puberty, adulthood and pregnancy. It also has important effects in many other tissues including bone, fat, skin, liver, and the brain.

questions.explanation: LH causes the ovulation and secretion of progesterone and oestrogen from ovarian follicles and corpus luteum. It is secreted by the anterior pituitary lobe at the end of the proliferative stage (day 14). In response to sudden release of LH from anterior pituitary lobe, egg from the mature follicle is released into the oviduct. The event is known as ovulation.

questions.text: According to the accepted concept of hormone action, if receptor molecules are removed from target organs, then the target organ will
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Continue to respond to the hormone without any difference
options.explanation: The molecules of hormones that are amino acid derivatives, peptides or proteins are large and insoluble in lipids, and cannot enter the target cell. Therefore, they act at the cell surface. They bind to specific receptor molecules located on the surface of the cell membrane. Therefore, if receptor molecules are removed from target organs, then the target organ will not respond to the hormone.

The molecules of hormones that are amino acid derivatives, peptides or proteins are large and insoluble in lipids, and cannot enter the target cell. Therefore, they act at the cell surface. They bind to specific receptor molecules located on the surface of the cell membrane. Therefore, if receptor molecules are removed from target organs, then the target organ will not respond to the hormone.

options.text: Continue to respond to the hormone but will require higher concentration
options.explanation: The molecules of hormones that are amino acid derivatives, peptides or proteins are large and insoluble in lipids, and cannot enter the target cell. Therefore, they act at the cell surface. They bind to specific receptor molecules located on the surface of the cell membrane. Therefore, if receptor molecules are removed from target organs, then the target organ will not respond to the hormone.

options.text: Continue to respond to the hormone but in the opposite way
options.explanation: The molecules of hormones that are amino acid derivatives, peptides or proteins are large and insoluble in lipids, and cannot enter the target cell. Therefore, they act at the cell surface. They bind to specific receptor molecules located on the surface of the cell membrane. Therefore, if receptor molecules are removed from target organs, then the target organ will not respond to the hormone.

Therefore, they act at the cell surface. They bind to specific receptor molecules located on the surface of the cell membrane. Therefore, if receptor molecules are removed from target organs, then the target organ will not respond to the hormone.
